deposits.wiki_article: 'Diversity Contraction Across Substrates' establishes a boundary law for semantic exhaustion: the formal condition under which a regenerative system's diversity irreversibly collapses to a trap state. The paper derives the Mediation Ratchet (α* = p/g₀) as the closed-form analytic threshold governing when pruning overwhelms regeneration. An exogenous floor makes a system exhaustion-resistant; without one, diversity contracts monotonically. The paper is positioned upstream of the DS-6 measurement program and beneath the semantic-physics field map.

deposits.creator: Lee Sharks
deposits.description: Empirical falsification of DOI permanence. Of 1,817 DOIs audited, 871 return HTTP 404 from DataCite's public metadata API — complete public metadata erasure. 946 preserved, 871 severed, sum exact. Mints identifier severance: the persistence of an identifier string after the public semantic relation between the string and its referent has been removed. Includes executable verification procedure.

deposits.description: Companion paper to #868 (DOIs ≠ Permanent Identifiers). Uses the 2026-06-22 OpenAIRE helpdesk email as documentary anchor. Maps OpenAIRE's credibility wager across four publicly-stated commitments and documents the architectural gap between those commitments and the metadata-graph behavior that propagates link rot. Mints 'Governance Dissociation' as structural complement to Identifier Severance — the pattern where unified-branded FAIR infrastructure components disclaim responsibility for failures their architecture produces.
